description:
To properly set the permissions of /etc/ssh/sshd_config, run the command: 
$ sudo chmod 0600 /etc/ssh/sshd_config
rationale:
Service configuration files enable or disable features of their respective
services that if configured incorrectly can lead to insecure and vulnerable
configurations. Therefore, service configuration files should be owned by the
correct group to prevent unauthorized changes.
ocil:
To check the permissions of /etc/ssh/sshd_config, run the command: 
$ ls -l /etc/ssh/sshd_config
If properly configured, the output should indicate the following permissions: -rw-------
fixtext:
Configure the "/etc/ssh/sshd_config" file to "0600" by running the following command:
$ sudo chmod 0600 /etc/ssh/sshd_config
srg_requirement: The Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 /etc/ssh/sshd_config file must have mode 0600 or less permissive to prevent unauthorized access.

stig.srg_requirement: Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 SSH server configuration files' permissions must not be modified.
stig.vuldiscussion: Service configuration files enable or disable features of their respective services that if configured incorrectly can lead to insecure and vulnerable configurations. Therefore, service configuration files should be owned by the correct group to prevent unauthorized changes.
stig.checktext:
Verify the permissions of the "/etc/ssh/sshd_config" file with the following command:

$ sudo rpm --verify openssh-server

If the command returns any output, this is a finding.
stig.fixtext:
Run the following commands to restore the correct permissions of OpenSSH server configuration files:

$ sudo dnf reinstall -y openssh-server
$ rpm --setugids openssh-server
$ rpm --setperms openssh-server

title: Verify Permissions on SSH Server config file
